## 2026-03-20

### Spawn Model Mismatch - Said Kimi but used MiniMax

**Problem:** 口頭通知用戶準備 spawn Kimi sub-agent，但實際 spawn 指令冇指定 model 參數，導致使用默認 MiniMax

**Cause:** 冇執行檢查清單，口頭講同實際指令不一致

**Solution:** 加入防錯機制：執行 spawn 前必須檢查口頭講既 model 同指令一致

**Lesson:** 講到要做到！口頭講用咩 model，指令必須寫明 model 參數

*Source: session:f9321ca2-8f65-4fc1-94cc-6ae7d53dbeee | Type: Execution Error | ID: spawn-model-mismatch-001*

### Ollama Qwen3/3.5 Thinking Bug + MiniMax API Issue

**Problem:** L0/L1/Daily Summary 生成失敗既真正原因：
1. Qwen3/3.5 (包括 4b, 8b, 9b) 所有 output 都入晒 thinking 度，response 空
2. MiniMax direct API call 回傳 HTTP 401 invalid api key

**Cause:** 1. Ollama Qwen3 系列有 thinking mode bug，所有 tokens 都分配去 thinking
2. OpenClaw 內部既 MiniMax API key 同 direct API call 既認證方式唔同

**Solution:** 使用 qwen2.5:3b 代替 qwen3 系列 - qwen2.5 正常工作

**Lesson:** 之前一直以為係內容太長導致timeout，原來真正原因係 model 既 bug 同 API key 認證問題

*Source: system | Type: System Discovery | ID: ollama-qwen-thinking-1773514453996*

## 2026-03-07

### Disk 空間檢查錯誤 - 誤檢 Home Directory

**Problem:** 執行系統健康檢查時，使用 'df -h ~' 只檢查咗 home directory，而唔係整個系統。顯示 92% 使用率（17GB 剩餘），但實際系統係 35% 使用率（165GB 剩餘）。

**Cause:** 命令使用錯誤 - 'df -h ~' 只檢查特定目錄，應該用 'df -h /' 或系統級工具檢查。

**Solution:** 使用正確命令檢查整體系統空間：'df -h /' 或參考 macOS 系統顯示。

**Lesson:** 執行系統檢查時要確認命令檢查嘅範圍係咪正確，唔好假設單一目錄就代表整體系統狀態。

*Source: Issue Conversion | Type: Manual Entry | ID: mmfjlx4b8jrti*

## 2026-02-25

### Memory Filename Format Inconsistency

**Problem:** Discovered inconsistent filename formats across the system: (1) memory_temporal_search.js only searched for YYYY-MM-DD.md, missing new timestamped files (YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md) created after Feb 24. (2) log_to_daily_memory.js was writing to YYYY-MM-DD.md while other parts of system use YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.

**Cause:** System transition from date-only format (YYYY-MM-DD.md) to timestamped format (YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md) starting Feb 24 was incomplete. Not all scripts were updated to handle both formats. The log_to_daily_memory.js and memory_temporal_search.js were still using old format assumptions.

**Solution:** 1. Updated memory_temporal_search.js to support both YYYY-MM-DD.md and YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md formats with deduplication logic. 2. Updated log_to_daily_memory.js to use consistent timestamped format (HHMM suffix). 3. Added pattern /^d{4}-d{2}-d{2}.*.md$/ to match both formats. 4. Ensured backward compat

**Lesson:** When changing file naming conventions, MUST audit ALL scripts that read/write those files. Use consistent pattern matching: /^d{4}-d{2}-d{2}.*.md$/ to support both formats. Always test temporal search after file format changes.

*Source: Multiple Scripts | Type: System Error | ID: filename-format-inconsistency-1772034248893*

---

### L2 Memory File Read Failure - Filename Format Mismatch

**Problem:** L0/L1 Generators failed to read L2 Daily files. The generators looked for memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md but actual files are named memory/YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md (with timestamp). This caused file not found errors and resulted in empty L0/L1 summaries ("No activity recorded").

**Cause:** Filename format mismatch: Generator expected "2026-02-24.md" but actual file is "2026-02-24-0852.md". The session-memory system writes files with HHMM timestamp, but generators were hardcoded to look for date-only format.

**Solution:** 1. Updated generate_l1.js to use findLatestMemoryFile() function that searches for YYYY-MM-DD-*.md pattern and picks the latest file by modification time. 2. Updated generate_abstract.js with same fix. 3. Updated all related cron job messages to specify correct filename format. 4. Added documentatio

**Lesson:** ALWAYS verify filename patterns match between writer (session-memory) and reader (generators). When L0/L1 show "No activity recorded", first check if L2 files are being found correctly. L2 format: YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM.md, not YYYY-MM-DD.md.

*Source: L0/L1 Generator | Type: System Error | ID: l2-filename-mismatch-1772033448657*

### Forgot L0/L1/L2 Memory Architecture

**Problem:** User asked me to remember something from previous days. I didn't immediately recall the L0/L1/L2 memory architecture and had to be reminded to check it.

**Cause:** Did not automatically check L0/L1/L2 when memory was unclear. Relied on user's reminder instead of proactive retrieval. Context reset caused loss of recent session memory.

**Solution:** 1. Added explicit rule in AGENTS.md about memory retrieval. 2. Created checklist: L0 -> L1 -> L2 when uncertain. 3. Will proactively search memory before asking user.

**Lesson:** ALWAYS check L0/L1/L2 proactively when memory is unclear. Don't wait for user to remind me. L0 Abstract (200 words) -> L1 Overview (600 words) -> L2 Daily (raw logs).

*Source: User Feedback | Type: Memory Error | ID: mlzmem20250225*

---

### Cache Overflow: 283% Cache Ratio in Main Session

**Problem:** Main session (agent:main:main) showing 283% cache ratio with only 26k tokens. Other sessions also showing abnormal cache percentages (117%)

**Cause:** Cron jobs (L0/L1 generation at 00:05/00:35) executing in main session. Same content being cached multiple times without proper cleanup. Cache accumulation over time without garbage collection.

**Solution:** 1. Short-term: Run 'openclaw sessions prune' to clear
2. Long-term: Consider scheduling automatic prune weekly
3. Monitor for recurrence

**Lesson:** Monitor cache ratios regularly. Schedule periodic session pruning to prevent accumulation. Consider isolating cron jobs to separate sessions.

*Source: System Monitoring | Type: Performance Issue | ID: cache283main20250225*

### File Organization Error: Wrong Destination for Task

**Problem:** User proposed Obsidian integration scheme. I instinctively wrote it to MEMORY.md instead of creating an Issue in .issues/

**Cause:** 1. Session context limitation - didn't immediately recall Issue system\n2. Habit from previous MEMORY.md discussions\n3. Didn't proactively check for existing Issue structure\n4. No active verification step before deciding where to record

**Solution:** 1. Created Issue 017 in .issues/active/ with full proposal details\n2. Simplified MEMORY.md to reference the Issue instead\n3. Updated process to check .issues/ first for new tasks

**Lesson:** ALWAYS ask: Is this a new task or memory/knowledge? Task -> .issues/active/, Architecture/Knowledge -> MEMORY.md. Check existing Issue structure before deciding. When in doubt, default to Issue for actionable items.

*Source: User Feedback | Type: Process Error | ID: mlzq4x8process*

### Timezone Issue: UTC vs HKT

**Problem:** Multiple scripts using UTC time (toISOString) instead of HKT time, causing date discrepancies in logs and file naming

**Cause:** New scripts developed without consistent timezone handling. getHKTDate() helper not included in all scripts.

**Solution:** 1. All new scripts must include getHKTDate() function\n2. Run timezone_fixer.js periodically\n3. Include timezone check in code review

**Lesson:** Timezone handling is fundamental. Always consider timezone when developing date-related features.

*Source: Code Review | Type: System Pattern | ID: mlyl95tihbte5*

---

### Weekly correction loop duplicate execution

**Problem:** Weekly correction loop executed twice at 01:13 AM

**Cause:** Cron job timing overlap or duplicate scheduling

**Solution:** Need to adjust cron timing to prevent overlap

**Lesson:** Review cron job scheduling

*Source: Cron Job | Type: System Error | ID: mlyj0k9n7km2h*
